1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم حم
1.english: Ha, Meem.
1.transliteration: Haa-Meeem

1.arabic: تنزيل الكتاب من الله العزيز العليم
1.english: The revelation of the Book is from Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.
1.transliteration: Tanzeelul Kitaabi minal laahil Azeezil 'Aleem

1.arabic: غافر الذنب وقابل التوب شديد العقاب ذي الطول ۖ لا إله إلا هو ۖ إليه المصير
1.english: The forgiver of sin, acceptor of repentance, severe in punishment, owner of abundance. There is no deity except Him; to Him is the destination.
1.transliteration: Ghaafiriz zambi wa qaabilit tawbi shadeedil 'iqaabi zit tawli laaa ilaaha illaa Huwa ilaihil maseer

1.arabic: ما يجادل في آيات الله إلا الذين كفروا فلا يغررك تقلبهم في البلاد
1.english: No one disputes concerning the signs of Allah except those who disbelieve, so be not deceived by their [uninhibited] movement throughout the land.
1.transliteration: Maa yujaadilu feee Aayaatil laahi illal lazeena kafaroo falaa yaghrurka taqallubuhum fil bilaad

1.arabic: كذبت قبلهم قوم نوح والأحزاب من بعدهم ۖ وهمت كل أمة برسولهم ليأخذوه ۖ وجادلوا بالباطل ليدحضوا به الحق فأخذتهم ۖ فكيف كان عقاب
1.english: The people of Noah denied before them and the [disbelieving] factions after them, and every nation intended [a plot] for their messenger to seize him, and they disputed by [using] falsehood to [attempt to] invalidate thereby the truth. So I seized them, and how [terrible] was My penalty.
1.transliteration: Kazzabat qablahum qawmu Noohinw wal Ahzaabu mim ba'dihim wa hammat kullu ummatim bi Rasoolihim liyaa khuzoobhu wa jaadaloo bilbaatili liyudhidoo bihil haqqa fa akhaztuhum fakifa kaana 'iqaab

1.arabic: وكذلك حقت كلمت ربك على الذين كفروا أنهم أصحاب النار
1.english: And thus has the word of your Lord come into effect upon those who disbelieved that they are companions of the Fire.
1.transliteration: Wa kazaalika haqqat Kalimatu Rabbika 'alal lazeena kafarooo annahum Ashaabun Naar

1.arabic: الذين يحملون العرش ومن حوله يسبحون بحمد ربهم ويؤمنون به ويستغفرون للذين آمنوا ربنا وسعت كل شيء رحمة وعلما فاغفر للذين تابوا واتبعوا سبيلك وقهم عذاب الجحيم
1.english: Those [angels] who carry the Throne and those around it exalt [Allah] with praise of their Lord and believe in Him and ask forgiveness for those who have believed, [saying], "Our Lord, You have encompassed all things in mercy and knowledge, so forgive those who have repented and followed Your way and protect them from the punishment of Hellfire.
1.transliteration: Allazeena yahmiloonal 'Arsha wa man hawlahoo yusabbihoona bihamdi Rabbihim wa yu'minoona bihee wa yastaghfiroona lillazeena aamanoo Rabbanaa wasi'ta kulla shai'ir rahmantanw wa 'ilman faghfir lillazeena taaboo wattaba'oo sabeelaka wa qihim 'azaabal Jaheem

1.arabic: ربنا وأدخلهم جنات عدن التي وعدتهم ومن صلح من آبائهم وأزواجهم وذرياتهم ۚ إنك أنت العزيز الحكيم
1.english: Our Lord, and admit them to gardens of perpetual residence which You have promised them and whoever was righteous among their fathers, their spouses and their offspring. Indeed, it is You who is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
1.transliteration: Rabbannaa wa adkhilhum Jannaati 'adninil latee wa'attahum wa man salaha min aabaaa'ihim wa azwaajihim wa zurriyyaatihim; innaka Antal 'Azeezul Hakeem

1.arabic: وقهم السيئات ۚ ومن تق السيئات يومئذ فقد رحمته ۚ وذلك هو الفوز العظيم
1.english: And protect them from the evil consequences [of their deeds]. And he whom You protect from evil consequences that Day - You will have given him mercy. And that is the great attainment."
1.transliteration: Wa qihimus saiyi-aat; wa man taqis saiyi-aati Yawma'izin faqad rahimtah; wa zaalika huwal fawzul 'azeem

1.arabic: إن الذين كفروا ينادون لمقت الله أكبر من مقتكم أنفسكم إذ تدعون إلى الإيمان فتكفرون
1.english: Indeed, those who disbelieve will be addressed, "The hatred of Allah for you was [even] greater than your hatred of yourselves [this Day in Hell] when you were invited to faith, but you refused."
1.transliteration: Innal lazeena kafaroo yunaadawna lamaqtul laahi akbaru mim maqtikum anfusakum iz tud'awna ilal eemaani fatakfuroon

1.arabic: قالوا ربنا أمتنا اثنتين وأحييتنا اثنتين فاعترفنا بذنوبنا فهل إلى خروج من سبيل
1.english: They will say, "Our Lord, You made us lifeless twice and gave us life twice, and we have confessed our sins. So is there to an exit any way?"
1.transliteration: Qaaloo Rabbanaaa amat tanasnataini wa ahyaitanas nataini fa'tarafnaa bizunoo binaa fahal ilaa khuroojim min sabeel

1.arabic: ذلكم بأنه إذا دعي الله وحده كفرتم ۖ وإن يشرك به تؤمنوا ۚ فالحكم لله العلي الكبير
1.english: [They will be told], "That is because, when Allah was called upon alone, you disbelieved; but if others were associated with Him, you believed. So the judgement is with Allah, the Most High, the Grand."
1.transliteration: Zaalikum bi annahooo izaa du'iyal laahu wahdahoo kafartum wa iny yushrak bihee tu'minoo; falhukmu lillaahil 'Aliyyil Kabeer

1.arabic: هو الذي يريكم آياته وينزل لكم من السماء رزقا ۚ وما يتذكر إلا من ينيب
1.english: It is He who shows you His signs and sends down to you from the sky, provision. But none will remember except he who turns back [in repentance].
1.transliteration: Huwal lazee yureekum Aayaatihee wa yunazzilu lakum minas samaaa'i rizqaa; wa maa tatazakkaru illaa mai yuneeb

1.arabic: فادعوا الله مخلصين له الدين ولو كره الكافرون
1.english: So invoke Allah, [being] sincere to Him in religion, although the disbelievers dislike it.
1.transliteration: Fad'ul laaha mukhliseena lahud deena wa law karihal kaafiroon

1.arabic: رفيع الدرجات ذو العرش يلقي الروح من أمره على من يشاء من عباده لينذر يوم التلاق
1.english: [He is] the Exalted above [all] degrees, Owner of the Throne; He places the inspiration of His command upon whom He wills of His servants to warn of the Day of Meeting.
1.transliteration: Rafee'ud darajaati zul 'Arshi yulqir rooha min amrihee 'alaa mai yashaaa'u min 'ibaadihee liyunzira yawmat talaaq

1.arabic: يوم هم بارزون ۖ لا يخفى على الله منهم شيء ۚ لمن الملك اليوم ۖ لله الواحد القهار
1.english: The Day they come forth nothing concerning them will be concealed from Allah. To whom belongs [all] sovereignty this Day? To Allah, the One, the Prevailing.
1.transliteration: Yawma hum baarizoona laa yakhfaa 'alal laahi minhum shai; limanil mulkul Yawma lillaahil Waahidil Qahaar

1.arabic: اليوم تجزى كل نفس بما كسبت ۚ لا ظلم اليوم ۚ إن الله سريع الحساب
1.english: This Day every soul will be recompensed for what it earned. No injustice today! Indeed, Allah is swift in account.
1.transliteration: Al-Yawma tujzaa kullu nafsim bimaa kasabat; laa zulmal Yawm; innal laaha saree'ul hisaab

1.arabic: وأنذرهم يوم الآزفة إذ القلوب لدى الحناجر كاظمين ۚ ما للظالمين من حميم ولا شفيع يطاع
1.english: And warn them, [O Muhammad], of the Approaching Day, when hearts are at the throats, filled [with distress]. For the wrongdoers there will be no devoted friend and no intercessor [who is] obeyed.
1.transliteration: Wa anzirhum yawmal aazifati izil quloobu ladal hanaajiri kaazimeen; maa lizzaalimeena min hameeminw wa laa shafee'iny-yutaa'

1.arabic: يعلم خائنة الأعين وما تخفي الصدور
1.english: He knows that which deceives the eyes and what the breasts conceal.
1.transliteration: Ya'lamu khaaa'inatal a'yuni wa maa tukhfis sudoor

1.arabic: والله يقضي بالحق ۖ والذين يدعون من دونه لا يقضون بشيء ۗ إن الله هو السميع البصير
1.english: And Allah judges with truth, while those they invoke besides Him judge not with anything. Indeed, Allah - He is the Hearing, the Seeing.
1.transliteration: Wallaahu yaqdee bilhaqq, wallazeena yad'oona min doonihee laa yaqdoona bishai'; innal laaha Huwas Samee'ul Baseer

1.arabic: ۞ أولم يسيروا في الأرض فينظروا كيف كان عاقبة الذين كانوا من قبلهم ۚ كانوا هم أشد منهم قوة وآثارا في الأرض فأخذهم الله بذنوبهم وما كان لهم من الله من واق
1.english: Have they not traveled through the land and observed how was the end of those who were before them? They were greater than them in strength and in impression on the land, but Allah seized them for their sins. And they had not from Allah any protector.
1.transliteration: Awalam yaseeroo fil ardi fa yanzuroo kaifa kaana 'aaqibatul lazeena kaanoo min qablihim; kaanoo hum ashadda minhum quwwatanw wa aasaaran fil ardi fa akhazahumul laahu bizunoobihim wa maa kaana lahum minal laahi minw waaq

1.arabic: ذلك بأنهم كانت تأتيهم رسلهم بالبينات فكفروا فأخذهم الله ۚ إنه قوي شديد العقاب
1.english: That was because their messengers were coming to them with clear proofs, but they disbelieved, so Allah seized them. Indeed, He is Powerful and severe in punishment.
1.transliteration: Zaalika bi annahum kaanat taateehim Rusuluhum bilbaiyinaati fakafaroo fa akhazahumul laah; innahoo qawiyyun shadeedul 'iqaab

1.arabic: ولقد أرسلنا موسى بآياتنا وسلطان مبين
1.english: And We did certainly send Moses with Our signs and a clear authority
1.transliteration: Wa laqad arsalnaa Moosaa bi Aayaatinaa wa sultaanim mubeen

1.arabic: إلى فرعون وهامان وقارون فقالوا ساحر كذاب
1.english: To Pharaoh, Haman and Qarun; but they said, "[He is] a magician and a liar."
1.transliteration: Ilaa Fir'awna wa Haamaana qa Qaaroona faqaaloo saahirun kazzaab

1.arabic: فلما جاءهم بالحق من عندنا قالوا اقتلوا أبناء الذين آمنوا معه واستحيوا نساءهم ۚ وما كيد الكافرين إلا في ضلال
1.english: And when he brought them the truth from Us, they said, "Kill the sons of those who have believed with him and keep their women alive." But the plan of the disbelievers is not except in error.
1.transliteration: Falamuna jaaa'ahum bil haqqi min 'indinaa qaaluq tulooo abnaaa'al lazeena aamanoo ma'ahoo wastahyoo nisaaa'ahum; wa maa kaidul kaafireena illaa fee dalaal

1.arabic: وقال فرعون ذروني أقتل موسى وليدع ربه ۖ إني أخاف أن يبدل دينكم أو أن يظهر في الأرض الفساد
1.english: And Pharaoh said, "Let me kill Moses and let him call upon his Lord. Indeed, I fear that he will change your religion or that he will cause corruption in the land."
1.transliteration: Wa qaala Fir'awnu zarooneee aqtul Moosaa walyad'u Rabbahoo inneee akhaafu ai yubaddila deenakum aw ai yuzhira fil ardil fasaad

1.arabic: وقال موسى إني عذت بربي وربكم من كل متكبر لا يؤمن بيوم الحساب
1.english: But Moses said, "Indeed, I have sought refuge in my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant one who does not believe in the Day of Account."
1.transliteration: Wa qaala Moosaaaa innee 'uztu bi Rabbee wa Rabbikum min kulli mutakabbiril laayu'minu bi Yawmil Hisaab

1.arabic: وقال رجل مؤمن من آل فرعون يكتم إيمانه أتقتلون رجلا أن يقول ربي الله وقد جاءكم بالبينات من ربكم ۖ وإن يك كاذبا فعليه كذبه ۖ وإن يك صادقا يصبكم بعض الذي يعدكم ۖ إن الله لا يهدي من هو مسرف كذاب
1.english: And a believing man from the family of Pharaoh who concealed his faith said, "Do you kill a man [merely] because he says, 'My Lord is Allah' while he has brought you clear proofs from your Lord? And if he should be lying, then upon him is [the consequence of] his lie; but if he should be truthful, there will strike you some of what he promises you. Indeed, Allah does not guide one who is a transgressor and a liar.
1.transliteration: Wa qaala rajulum mu'minummin Aali Fir'awna yaktumu eemaanahooo ataqtuloona rajulan ai yaqoola Rabbi yal laahu wa qad jaaa'akum bil haiyinaati mir Rabbikum wa iny yaku kaaziban fa'alaihi kazi buhoo wa iny yaku saadiqany yasibkum ba'dul lazee ya'idukum innal laaha laa yahdee man huwa musrifun kaazaab

1.arabic: يا قوم لكم الملك اليوم ظاهرين في الأرض فمن ينصرنا من بأس الله إن جاءنا ۚ قال فرعون ما أريكم إلا ما أرى وما أهديكم إلا سبيل الرشاد
1.english: O my people, sovereignty is yours today, [your being] dominant in the land. But who would protect us from the punishment of Allah if it came to us?" Pharaoh said, "I do not show you except what I see, and I do not guide you except to the way of right conduct."
1.transliteration: Yaa qawmi lakumul mulkul yawma zaahireena fil ardi famai yansurunaa mim baasil laahi in jaaa'anaa; qaala Fir'awnu maaa ureekum illaa maaa araa wa maaa ahdeekum illaa sabeelar Rashaad

1.arabic: وقال الذي آمن يا قوم إني أخاف عليكم مثل يوم الأحزاب
1.english: And he who believed said, "O my people, indeed I fear for you [a fate] like the day of the companies -
1.transliteration: Wa qaalal lazee aamana yaa qawmi inneee akhaafu 'alaikum misla yawmil Ahzaab

1.arabic: مثل دأب قوم نوح وعاد وثمود والذين من بعدهم ۚ وما الله يريد ظلما للعباد
1.english: Like the custom of the people of Noah and of 'Aad and Thamud and those after them. And Allah wants no injustice for [His] servants.
1.transliteration: Misla daabi qawmi Noohinw wa 'aadinw wa Samooda wallazeena mim ba'dihim; wa mal laahu yureedu zulmal lil'ibaad

1.arabic: ويا قوم إني أخاف عليكم يوم التناد
1.english: And O my people, indeed I fear for you the Day of Calling -
1.transliteration: Wa yaa qawmi inneee akhaafu 'alaikum yawmat tanaad

1.arabic: يوم تولون مدبرين ما لكم من الله من عاصم ۗ ومن يضلل الله فما له من هاد
1.english: The Day you will turn your backs fleeing; there is not for you from Allah any protector. And whoever Allah leaves astray - there is not for him any guide.
1.transliteration: Yawma tuwalloona mud bireena maa lakum minal laahi min 'aasim; wa mai yudlilil laahu famaa lahoo min haad

1.arabic: ولقد جاءكم يوسف من قبل بالبينات فما زلتم في شك مما جاءكم به ۖ حتى إذا هلك قلتم لن يبعث الله من بعده رسولا ۚ كذلك يضل الله من هو مسرف مرتاب
1.english: And Joseph had already come to you before with clear proofs, but you remained in doubt of that which he brought to you, until when he died, you said, 'Never will Allah send a messenger after him.' Thus does Allah leave astray he who is a transgressor and skeptic."
1.transliteration: Wa laqad jaaa'akum Yoosufu min qablu bil baiyinaati famaa ziltum fee shakkim mimaa jaaa'akum bihee hattaaa izaa halaka qultum lai yab asal laahu mim ba'dihee Rasoolaa; kazaalika yudillul laahu man huwa Musrifum murtaab

1.arabic: الذين يجادلون في آيات الله بغير سلطان أتاهم ۖ كبر مقتا عند الله وعند الذين آمنوا ۚ كذلك يطبع الله على كل قلب متكبر جبار
1.english: Those who dispute concerning the signs of Allah without an authority having come to them - great is hatred [of them] in the sight of Allah and in the sight of those who have believed. Thus does Allah seal over every heart [belonging to] an arrogant tyrant.
1.transliteration: Allazeena yujaadiloona feee Aaayaatil laahi bighairi sultaanin ataahum kabura maqtan 'indal laahi wa 'indal lazeena aamanoo; kazaalika yatha'ul laahu 'alaa kulli qalbi mutakabbirin jabbaar

1.arabic: وقال فرعون يا هامان ابن لي صرحا لعلي أبلغ الأسباب
1.english: And Pharaoh said, "O Haman, construct for me a tower that I might reach the ways -
1.transliteration: Wa qaala Fir'awnu yaa Haamaanub-ni lee sarhal la'alleee ablughul asbaab

1.arabic: أسباب السماوات فأطلع إلى إله موسى وإني لأظنه كاذبا ۚ وكذلك زين لفرعون سوء عمله وصد عن السبيل ۚ وما كيد فرعون إلا في تباب
1.english: The ways into the heavens - so that I may look at the deity of Moses; but indeed, I think he is a liar." And thus was made attractive to Pharaoh the evil of his deed, and he was averted from the [right] way. And the plan of Pharaoh was not except in ruin.
1.transliteration: Asbaabas samaawaati faattali'a ilaaa ilaahi Moosaa wa innee la azunnuhoo kaazibaa; wa kazaalika zuyyina li-Fir'awna sooo'u 'amalihee wa sudda 'anis sabeel; wa maa kaidu Fir'awna illaa fee tabaab

1.arabic: وقال الذي آمن يا قوم اتبعون أهدكم سبيل الرشاد
1.english: And he who believed said, "O my people, follow me, I will guide you to the way of right conduct.
1.transliteration: Wa qaalal lazeee aamana yaa qawmit tabi'ooni ahdikum sabeelar rashaad

1.arabic: يا قوم إنما هذه الحياة الدنيا متاع وإن الآخرة هي دار القرار
1.english: O my people, this worldly life is only [temporary] enjoyment, and indeed, the Hereafter - that is the home of [permanent] settlement.
1.transliteration: Yaa qawmi innamaa haazihil hayaatud dunyaa mataa'unw wa innal Aakhirata hiya daarul qaraar

1.arabic: من عمل سيئة فلا يجزى إلا مثلها ۖ ومن عمل صالحا من ذكر أو أنثى وهو مؤمن فأولئك يدخلون الجنة يرزقون فيها بغير حساب
1.english: Whoever does an evil deed will not be recompensed except by the like thereof; but whoever does righteousness, whether male or female, while he is a believer - those will enter Paradise, being given provision therein without account.
1.transliteration: Man 'amila saiyi'atan falaa yujzaaa illaa mislahaa wa man 'amila saaliham min zakarin aw unsaa wa huwa mu'minun fa ulaaa'ika yadkhuloonal Jannata yurzaqoona feehaa bighairi hisaab

1.arabic: ۞ ويا قوم ما لي أدعوكم إلى النجاة وتدعونني إلى النار
1.english: And O my people, how is it that I invite you to salvation while you invite me to the Fire?
1.transliteration: Wa yaa qawmi maa leee ad'ookum ilan najaati wa tad'oonaneee ilan Naar

1.arabic: تدعونني لأكفر بالله وأشرك به ما ليس لي به علم وأنا أدعوكم إلى العزيز الغفار
1.english: You invite me to disbelieve in Allah and associate with Him that of which I have no knowledge, and I invite you to the Exalted in Might, the Perpetual Forgiver.
1.transliteration: Tad'oonanee li-akfura billaahi wa ushrika bihee maa laisa lee bihee 'ilmunw wa ana ad'ookum ilal'Azeezil Ghaffaar

1.arabic: لا جرم أنما تدعونني إليه ليس له دعوة في الدنيا ولا في الآخرة وأن مردنا إلى الله وأن المسرفين هم أصحاب النار
1.english: Assuredly, that to which you invite me has no [response to a] supplication in this world or in the Hereafter; and indeed, our return is to Allah, and indeed, the transgressors will be companions of the Fire.
1.transliteration: Laa jarama annamaa tad'oonanee ilaihi laisa lahoo da'watun fid dunyaa wa laa fil Aakhirati wa anna maraddanaaa ilal laahi wa annal musrifeenahum Ashaabun Naar

1.arabic: فستذكرون ما أقول لكم ۚ وأفوض أمري إلى الله ۚ إن الله بصير بالعباد
1.english: And you will remember what I [now] say to you, and I entrust my affair to Allah. Indeed, Allah is Seeing of [His] servants."
1.transliteration: Fasatazkuroona maaa aqoolu lakum; wa ufawwidu amreee ilal laah; innallaaha baseerum bil'ibaad

1.arabic: فوقاه الله سيئات ما مكروا ۖ وحاق بآل فرعون سوء العذاب
1.english: So Allah protected him from the evils they plotted, and the people of Pharaoh were enveloped by the worst of punishment -
1.transliteration: Fa waqaahul laahu saiyiaati maa makaroo wa haaqa bi Aali-Fir'awna sooo'ul 'azaab

1.arabic: النار يعرضون عليها غدوا وعشيا ۖ ويوم تقوم الساعة أدخلوا آل فرعون أشد العذاب
1.english: The Fire, they are exposed to it morning and evening. And the Day the Hour appears [it will be said], "Make the people of Pharaoh enter the severest punishment."
1.transliteration: An Naaru yu'radoona 'alaihaa ghuduwwanw wa 'ashiyyanw wa Yawma taqoomus Saa'aatu adkhilooo Aala Fir'awna ashaddal 'azaab

1.arabic: وإذ يتحاجون في النار فيقول الضعفاء للذين استكبروا إنا كنا لكم تبعا فهل أنتم مغنون عنا نصيبا من النار
1.english: And [mention] when they will argue within the Fire, and the weak will say to those who had been arrogant, "Indeed, we were [only] your followers, so will you relieve us of a share of the Fire?"
1.transliteration: Wa iz yatahaaajjoona fin Naari fa-yaqoolud du'afaaa'u lillazeenas takbarooo innaa kunnaa lakum taba'an fahal antum mughnoona annaa naseebam minan Naar

1.arabic: قال الذين استكبروا إنا كل فيها إن الله قد حكم بين العباد
1.english: Those who had been arrogant will say, "Indeed, all [of us] are in it. Indeed, Allah has judged between the servants."
1.transliteration: Qaalal lazeenas takbarooo innaa kullun feehaaa innal laaha qad hakama baynal'ibaad

1.arabic: وقال الذين في النار لخزنة جهنم ادعوا ربكم يخفف عنا يوما من العذاب
1.english: And those in the Fire will say to the keepers of Hell, "Supplicate your Lord to lighten for us a day from the punishment."
1.transliteration: Wa qaalal lazena fin Naari likhazanati Jahannamad-'oo Rabbakum yukhaffif 'annaa yawmam minal 'azaab

1.arabic: قالوا أولم تك تأتيكم رسلكم بالبينات ۖ قالوا بلى ۚ قالوا فادعوا ۗ وما دعاء الكافرين إلا في ضلال
1.english: They will say, "Did there not come to you your messengers with clear proofs?" They will say, "Yes." They will reply, "Then supplicate [yourselves], but the supplication of the disbelievers is not except in error."
1.transliteration: Qaalooo awalam taku taateekum Rusulukum bilbaiyinaati qaaloo balaa' qaaloo fad'oo; wa maa du'aaa'ul kaafireena illaa fee dalaal

1.arabic: إنا لننصر رسلنا والذين آمنوا في الحياة الدنيا ويوم يقوم الأشهاد
1.english: Indeed, We will support Our messengers and those who believe during the life of this world and on the Day when the witnesses will stand -
1.transliteration: Innaa lanansuru Rusulanaa wallazeena aamanoo fil hayaatid dunyaa wa Yawma yaqoomul ashhaad

1.arabic: يوم لا ينفع الظالمين معذرتهم ۖ ولهم اللعنة ولهم سوء الدار
1.english: The Day their excuse will not benefit the wrongdoers, and they will have the curse, and they will have the worst home.
1.transliteration: Yawma laa yanfa'uz zaalimeena ma'ziratuhum wa lahumul la'natu wa lahum soooud daar

1.arabic: ولقد آتينا موسى الهدى وأورثنا بني إسرائيل الكتاب
1.english: And We had certainly given Moses guidance, and We caused the Children of Israel to inherit the Scripture
1.transliteration: Wa laqad aatainaa Moosal hudaa wa awrasnaa Baneee Israaa 'eelal Kitaab

1.arabic: هدى وذكرى لأولي الألباب
1.english: As guidance and a reminder for those of understanding.
1.transliteration: Hudanw wa zikraa li ulil albaab

1.arabic: فاصبر إن وعد الله حق واستغفر لذنبك وسبح بحمد ربك بالعشي والإبكار
1.english: So be patient, [O Muhammad]. Indeed, the promise of Allah is truth. And ask forgiveness for your sin and exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord in the evening and the morning.
1.transliteration: Fasbir inna wa'dal laahi haqqunw wastaghfir lizambika wa sabbih bihamdi Rabbika bil'ashiyyi wal ibkaar

1.arabic: إن الذين يجادلون في آيات الله بغير سلطان أتاهم ۙ إن في صدورهم إلا كبر ما هم ببالغيه ۚ فاستعذ بالله ۖ إنه هو السميع البصير
1.english: Indeed, those who dispute concerning the signs of Allah without [any] authority having come to them - there is not within their breasts except pride, [the extent of] which they cannot reach. So seek refuge in Allah. Indeed, it is He who is the Hearing, the Seeing.
1.transliteration: Innal lazeena yujaadi loona feee Aayaatil laahi bighairi sultaanin ataahum in fee sudoorihim illaa kibrum maa hum bibaaligheeh; fasta'iz billaahi innahoo Huwas Samee'ul Baseer

1.arabic: لخلق السماوات والأرض أكبر من خلق الناس ولكن أكثر الناس لا يعلمون
1.english: The creation of the heavens and earth is greater than the creation of mankind, but most of the people do not know.
1.transliteration: Lakhalqus samaawaati wal ardi akbaru min khalqin naasi wa laakinna aksaran naasi laa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: وما يستوي الأعمى والبصير والذين آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات ولا المسيء ۚ قليلا ما تتذكرون
1.english: And not equal are the blind and the seeing, nor are those who believe and do righteous deeds and the evildoer. Little do you remember.
1.transliteration: Wa maa yastawil a'maa walbaseeru wallazeena aamanoo wa 'amilus saalihaati wa lal museee'; qaleelam maa tatazakkaroon

1.arabic: إن الساعة لآتية لا ريب فيها ولكن أكثر الناس لا يؤمنون
1.english: Indeed, the Hour is coming - no doubt about it - but most of the people do not believe.
1.transliteration: Innas Saa'ata la aatiyatul laa raiba feehaa wa laakinna aksaran naasi laa yu'minoon

1.arabic: وقال ربكم ادعوني أستجب لكم ۚ إن الذين يستكبرون عن عبادتي سيدخلون جهنم داخرين
1.english: And your Lord says, "Call upon Me; I will respond to you." Indeed, those who disdain My worship will enter Hell [rendered] contemptible.
1.transliteration: Wa qaala Rabbukumud 'ooneee astajib lakum; innal lazeena yastakbiroona an 'ibaadatee sa yadkhuloona jahannama daakhireen

1.arabic: الله الذي جعل لكم الليل لتسكنوا فيه والنهار مبصرا ۚ إن الله لذو فضل على الناس ولكن أكثر الناس لا يشكرون
1.english: It is Allah who made for you the night that you may rest therein and the day giving sight. Indeed, Allah is full of bounty to the people, but most of the people are not grateful.
1.transliteration: Allaahul lazee ja'ala lakumul laila litqaskunoo feehi wannahaara mubsiraa; innal laaha lazoo fadlin 'alan naasi wa laakinna aksaran naasi laa yashkuroon

1.arabic: ذلكم الله ربكم خالق كل شيء لا إله إلا هو ۖ فأنى تؤفكون
1.english: That is Allah, your Lord, Creator of all things; there is no deity except Him, so how are you deluded?
1.transliteration: Zaalikumul laahu Rabbukum khaaliqu kulli shai'; laaa ilaaha illaa Huwa fa annaa tu'fakoon

1.arabic: كذلك يؤفك الذين كانوا بآيات الله يجحدون
1.english: Thus were those [before you] deluded who were rejecting the signs of Allah.
1.transliteration: Kazaalika yu'fakul loazeena kaanoo bi Aayaatil laahi yajhadoon

1.arabic: الله الذي جعل لكم الأرض قرارا والسماء بناء وصوركم فأحسن صوركم ورزقكم من الطيبات ۚ ذلكم الله ربكم ۖ فتبارك الله رب العالمين
1.english: It is Allah who made for you the earth a place of settlement and the sky a ceiling and formed you and perfected your forms and provided you with good things. That is Allah, your Lord; then blessed is Allah, Lord of the worlds.
1.transliteration: Allaahul lazee ja'ala lakumul arda qaraaranw wassa maaa'a binaaa'anw wa sawwarakum fa ahsana suwarakum wa razaqakum minat taiyibaat; zaalikumul laahu Rabbukum fatabaarakal laahu Rabbul 'aalameen

1.arabic: هو الحي لا إله إلا هو فادعوه مخلصين له الدين ۗ الحمد لله رب العالمين
1.english: He is the Ever-Living; there is no deity except Him, so call upon Him, [being] sincere to Him in religion. [All] praise is [due] to Allah, Lord of the worlds.
1.transliteration: Huwal Hayyu laaa ilaaha illaa Huwa fad'oohu mukh liseena lahudeen; alhamdu lillaahi Rabbil 'aalameen

1.arabic: ۞ قل إني نهيت أن أعبد الذين تدعون من دون الله لما جاءني البينات من ربي وأمرت أن أسلم لرب العالمين
1.english: Say, [O Muhammad], "Indeed, I have been forbidden to worship those you call upon besides Allah once the clear proofs have come to me from my Lord, and I have been commanded to submit to the Lord of the worlds."
1.transliteration: Qul innee nuheetu an a'budal lazeena tad'oona min doonil laahi lammaa jaaa'a niyal biyinaatu mir Rabbee wa umirtu an uslima li Rabbil 'aalameen

1.arabic: هو الذي خلقكم من تراب ثم من نطفة ثم من علقة ثم يخرجكم طفلا ثم لتبلغوا أشدكم ثم لتكونوا شيوخا ۚ ومنكم من يتوفى من قبل ۖ ولتبلغوا أجلا مسمى ولعلكم تعقلون
1.english: It is He who created you from dust, then from a sperm-drop, then from a clinging clot; then He brings you out as a child; then [He develops you] that you reach your [time of] maturity, then [further] that you become elders. And among you is he who is taken in death before [that], so that you reach a specified term; and perhaps you will use reason.
1.transliteration: Huwal lazee khalaqakum min turaabin summa min nutfatin summa min 'alaqatin summa yukhrijukum tiflan summa litablughooo ashuddakum summa litakoonoo shuyookhaa; wa minkum mai yutawaffaa min qablu wa litablughooo ajalam musam manw-wa la'allakum ta'qiloon

1.arabic: هو الذي يحيي ويميت ۖ فإذا قضى أمرا فإنما يقول له كن فيكون
1.english: He it is who gives life and causes death; and when He decrees a matter, He but says to it, "Be," and it is.
1.transliteration: Huwal lazee yuhyee wa yumeetu fa izaa qadaaa amran fa innamaa yaqoolu lahoo kun fa yakon

1.arabic: ألم تر إلى الذين يجادلون في آيات الله أنى يصرفون
1.english: Do you not consider those who dispute concerning the signs of Allah - how are they averted?
1.transliteration: Alam tara ilal lazeena yujaadiloona feee Aayaatil laahi annaa yusrafoon

1.arabic: الذين كذبوا بالكتاب وبما أرسلنا به رسلنا ۖ فسوف يعلمون
1.english: Those who deny the Book and that with which We sent Our messengers - they are going to know,
1.transliteration: Allazeena kazzaboo bil Kitaabi wa bimaa arsalnaa bihee Rusulanaa fasawfa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: إذ الأغلال في أعناقهم والسلاسل يسحبون
1.english: When the shackles are around their necks and the chains; they will be dragged
1.transliteration: Izil aghlaalu feee a'naaqi-him wassalaasilu yashaboon

1.arabic: في الحميم ثم في النار يسجرون
1.english: In boiling water; then in the Fire they will be filled [with flame].
1.transliteration: Fil hameemi summa fin Naari Yasjaroon

1.arabic: ثم قيل لهم أين ما كنتم تشركون
1.english: Then it will be said to them, "Where is that which you used to associate [with Him in worship]
1.transliteration: Summaa qeela lahum ayna maa kuntum tushrikoon

1.arabic: من دون الله ۖ قالوا ضلوا عنا بل لم نكن ندعو من قبل شيئا ۚ كذلك يضل الله الكافرين
1.english: Other than Allah?" They will say, "They have departed from us; rather, we did not used to invoke previously anything." Thus does Allah put astray the disbelievers.
1.transliteration: Min doonil laahi qaaloo dalloo 'annaa bal lam nakun nad'oo min qablu shai'aa; kazaalika yudillul laahul kaafireen

1.arabic: ذلكم بما كنتم تفرحون في الأرض بغير الحق وبما كنتم تمرحون
1.english: [The angels will say], "That was because you used to exult upon the earth without right and you used to behave insolently.
1.transliteration: Zaalikum bimaa kuntum tafrahoona fil ardi bighairil haqqi wa bimaa kuntum tamrahoon

1.arabic: ادخلوا أبواب جهنم خالدين فيها ۖ فبئس مثوى المتكبرين
1.english: Enter the gates of Hell to abide eternally therein, and wretched is the residence of the arrogant."
1.transliteration: Udkhulooo abwaaba Jahannama khaalideena feehaa fabi'sa maswal mutakabbireen

1.arabic: فاصبر إن وعد الله حق ۚ فإما نرينك بعض الذي نعدهم أو نتوفينك فإلينا يرجعون
1.english: So be patient, [O Muhammad]; indeed, the promise of Allah is truth. And whether We show you some of what We have promised them or We take you in death, it is to Us they will be returned.
1.transliteration: Fasbir inna wa'dal laahi haqq; fa immaa nuriyannak ba'dal lazee na'i duhum aw natawaffayannaka fa ilainaa yurja'oon

1.arabic: ولقد أرسلنا رسلا من قبلك منهم من قصصنا عليك ومنهم من لم نقصص عليك ۗ وما كان لرسول أن يأتي بآية إلا بإذن الله ۚ فإذا جاء أمر الله قضي بالحق وخسر هنالك المبطلون
1.english: And We have already sent messengers before you. Among them are those [whose stories] We have related to you, and among them are those [whose stories] We have not related to you. And it was not for any messenger to bring a sign [or verse] except by permission of Allah. So when the command of Allah comes, it will be concluded in truth, and the falsifiers will thereupon lose [all].
1.transliteration: Wa laqad arsalnaa Rusulam min qablika minhum man qasasnaa 'alaika wa minhum mal lam naqsus 'alaik; wa maa kaana li Rasoolin any yaatiya bi Aayatin illaa bi iznil laah; fa izaa jaaa'a amrul laahi qudiya bilhaqqi wa khasira hunaalikal mubtiloon

1.arabic: الله الذي جعل لكم الأنعام لتركبوا منها ومنها تأكلون
1.english: It is Allah who made for you the grazing animals upon which you ride, and some of them you eat.
1.transliteration: Allaahul lazee ja'ala lakumul an'aama litarkaboo minhaa wa minhaa taakuloon

1.arabic: ولكم فيها منافع ولتبلغوا عليها حاجة في صدوركم وعليها وعلى الفلك تحملون
1.english: And for you therein are [other] benefits and that you may realize upon them a need which is in your breasts; and upon them and upon ships you are carried.
1.transliteration: Wa lakum feehaa manaafi'u wa litablughoo 'alaihaa haajatan fee sudoorikum wa 'alaihaa wa 'alal fulki tuhmaloon

1.arabic: ويريكم آياته فأي آيات الله تنكرون
1.english: And He shows you His signs. So which of the signs of Allah do you deny?
1.transliteration: Wa yureekum Aayaatihee fa ayya Aayaatil laahi tunkiroon

1.arabic: أفلم يسيروا في الأرض فينظروا كيف كان عاقبة الذين من قبلهم ۚ كانوا أكثر منهم وأشد قوة وآثارا في الأرض فما أغنى عنهم ما كانوا يكسبون
1.english: Have they not traveled through the land and observed how was the end of those before them? They were more numerous than themselves and greater in strength and in impression on the land, but they were not availed by what they used to earn.
1.transliteration: Afalam yaseeroo fil ardi fa yanzuroo kaifa kaana 'aaqibatul lazeena min qablihim; kaanoo aksara minhum wa ashadda quwwatanw wa aasaaran fil ardi famaaa aghnaa 'anhum maa kaanoo yaksiboon

1.arabic: فلما جاءتهم رسلهم بالبينات فرحوا بما عندهم من العلم وحاق بهم ما كانوا به يستهزئون
1.english: And when their messengers came to them with clear proofs, they [merely] rejoiced in what they had of knowledge, but they were enveloped by what they used to ridicule.
1.transliteration: Falammaa jaaa'at hum Rusuluhum bilbaiyinaati farihoo bimaa 'indahum minal 'ilmi wa haaqa bihim maa kaanoo bihee yastahzi'oon

1.arabic: فلما رأوا بأسنا قالوا آمنا بالله وحده وكفرنا بما كنا به مشركين
1.english: And when they saw Our punishment, they said," We believe in Allah alone and disbelieve in that which we used to associate with Him."
1.transliteration: Falammaa ra aw baasanaa qaalooo aamannaa billaahi wahdahoo wa kafarnaa bimaa kunnaa bihee mushrikeen

1.arabic: فلم يك ينفعهم إيمانهم لما رأوا بأسنا ۖ سنت الله التي قد خلت في عباده ۖ وخسر هنالك الكافرون
1.english: But never did their faith benefit them once they saw Our punishment. [It is] the established way of Allah which has preceded among His servants. And the disbelievers thereupon lost [all].
1.transliteration: Falam yaku tanfa 'uhum eemaanuhum lammaa ra-aw baasana sunnatal laahil latee qad khalat fee 'ibaadihee wa khasira hunaalikal kaafiroon
